mnemonics.title: Remembering Quantifiers
mnemonics.content: PAPA: 'For ALL' = For ALL, 'there EXISTS' = There EXISTS. P for Partial (some), A for All, A for All, P for Partial.

mnemonics.title: Inference Rules
mnemonics.content: MP: Modus Ponens = 'P implies Q, and P is true, so Q is true' (forward motion). MT: Modus Tollens = 'P implies Q, and Q is false, so P is false' (tollens means 'to deny').

acronyms.usage: Logic with quantifiers and predicates for representing relationships between objects
A clause represented as conjunction of disjunctions, used in resolution-based theorem proving
Database of facts and rules used by inference engine for logical reasoning
A syntactically correct formula in formal logic that follows grammar rules
Clause with at most one positive literal, efficient for automated reasoning

visualTips.visualization: Think of ∀ as an upside-down A (ALL have property) and ∃ as reversed E (EXISTS at least one). Draw a big circle for domain, shade entire circle for ∀, place single dot for ∃.
Forward: Facts → Rules → New Facts (arrow moves forward →). Backward: Goal ← Rules ← Facts (arrow points back to facts). Like reading a story vs solving a mystery from the ending.
Think of unification as pattern matching: Like(x, y) matching Like(John, Mary) by filling x=John, y=Mary. Use substitution as 'stamps' putting values into variable slots.

analogies.analogy: Propositional Logic is like describing a photo as 'black' or 'white' - limited. FOL is like describing every object, person, and relationship in the photo - much more detailed and expressive.
The inference engine is like a detective - it takes known facts (clues) and applies rules (detective methods) to deduce new information (solve the case).
Resolution is like 'cancelling out' opposite terms in math. Just as +5 and -5 cancel, literal Q and ¬Q resolve to produce the rest. Like solving an equation by eliminating common terms.
